agent_outputs.raw: The comparison is between two non-overlapping calendar periods that differ in a significant seasonal way. Tax-refund season (Feb–Apr) produces a known uplift in delinquency recoveries that has nothing to do with how accounts are ranked. Additionally, the simultaneous introduction of a new dialer vendor that expanded the reachable account population changes what accounts agents are even working — a higher-contactability pool will show higher recoveries per account regardless of ranking algorithm. The memo's causal claim is not supported. A concurrent champion-challenger test is the minimum required to draw any model-specific conclusion.

Four confounds are bundled together in this before-after comparison: seasonal liquidity effects, dialer-driven contactability improvement, manager override contamination, and the absence of a same-period control. Any one of these would make causal attribution uncertain; together they make it impossible. The 16% number is real but uninterpretable as a model-specific effect. The board should be told that the combined system shows promise and that a controlled evaluation is needed before crediting the model.

The critical flaw is the lack of a contemporaneous control group. Without knowing what recovery would have been in Feb–Apr under the old queue rules — with the same dialer, the same override policy, and the same seasonal conditions — the 16% figure could reflect entirely non-model factors. The seasonal effect alone (tax-refund liquidity) is a plausible full explanation for the observed gain. The additional dialer change further confounds the picture. The result is not informative about model quality without a concurrent comparison.

synthesis: All three assessments independently identify the same core problem: the before-after design with multiple simultaneous changes (seasonality, dialer, overrides) and no contemporaneous control cannot support a model-specific causal claim. The issues are genuine and empirically resolvable only through a properly controlled concurrent test.

empirical_test.condition: Concurrent randomized champion-challenger: model-ranked versus legacy-ranked queues in same calendar period, same dialer, tracked overrides
empirical_test.supports_critique_if: No material difference in recovery between conditions in concurrent test
empirical_test.supports_defense_if: Model-ranked condition outperforms legacy-ranked condition materially in the concurrent test
empirical_test.ambiguous_if: Small positive model effect with most of the 16% explained by seasonal and dialer factors
